exemplar.title: How does Adidas' adoption of CSR practices impact its brand image and financial performance?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Adidas' adoption of CSR practices impact its brand image and financial performance?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent foregrounding of sustainability as the key concept throughout the IA
Clear analytical linkage between sustainability, CSR initiatives and brand image
Explicit integration in research question, perception map axes and TBL analysis
weaknesses: Financialâratio commentary occasionally drifts into generic profitability discussion without explicit sustainability linkage
Integration of sustainability in people and planet dimensions could be more pervasive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in band 4: there is a strong, clear understanding of sustainability and good organisational connection, but occasional lapses in analytical integration prevent a topâband 5. The final score of 4 reflects this high but not flawless performance.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents provided, meeting the 3â5 requirement
Documents are directly relevant and sufficiently inâdepth (financial statements, survey data, academic and corporate sources)
Range of stakeholder perspectives (primary data, scholarly articles, corporate report) enhances depth
weaknesses: Secondary research sources lack full database or journal details, reducing transparency
Survey documentation omits administration date and platform
Summary of academic sources lacks inâtext author citations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA meets topâband requirements in both number (3â5 documents) and relevance/depth. Minor referencing and transparency issues do not undermine the overall quality; hence a full score of 4 is justified.

strengths: Appropriate selection of both qualitative (perception map, TBL) and quantitative (financial ratios) tools
Correct application of tools with clear linkages to brand image and performance
Effective use of outputs in discussion of CSR and marketing implications
weaknesses: Range of tools is moderateâlacks advanced strategic models or triangulation methods
CSR analysis could be strengthened with quantitative KPIs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection is adequate rather than outstanding (band 3) while application is effective. Given the moderate tool range and absence of advanced models, an overall score of 3 aligns with strand guidance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses multiâyear financial data, survey results and CSR metrics to support analysis
Logical reasoning linking CSR actions to profitability shifts and reputation factors
Contextual integration of the Kanye West controversy adds depth
weaknesses: Evaluative depth is sometimes superficial, with limited causality vs. correlation discussion
Small, skewed survey sample limits representativeness
Omissions in benchmarking purchase frequency and calibration details for perception map axes
Analysis occasionally overstates causality without sufficient evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there is clear analytical effort, numerous surfaceâlevel evaluations and methodological gaps cap the work at band 3 rather than 4. The score reflects solid but not consistently thorough analysis and evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions synthesize survey insights and financial trends coherently
Statements are broadly consistent with the evidence presented
Research question is explicitly answered and actionable recommendations are provided
weaknesses: Some conclusions over-generalize causal links without full evidentiary support
Recommendations lack detailed specificity regarding channels and targets
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion fully addresses the research question and aligns with the evidence (band 3). Minor over-generalizations and recommendation vagueness do not detract from its overall coherence.

strengths: IA follows the required structure with clear sections (title, contents, methodology, etc.)
Headings and tables are well organized, facilitating logical flow
weaknesses: Inconsistent section numbering in the table of contents
Research question could be more precisely defined to focus analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is appropriate throughout and meets topâband criteria (band 2). Minor numbering inconsistencies and scope precision issues are insufficient to lower the score.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (word count, bibliography, TOC, labeled figures)
Consistent styling of the Table of Contents and labeling of tables/figures
weaknesses: Formatting errors in citations (OCR artifacts) disrupt clarity
Table captions appear below tables and some figures are unlabeled
Notation for financial ratios deviates from standard formats
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the highest band (band 2) by including all required elements. Formatting and labeling issues are relatively minor and do not compromise overall readability.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows the required structureâtitle page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography and appendicesâfacilitating logical flow for the reader. Headings and tables are clear, so âappropriate structure throughoutâ is achieved (band 2).

Each document is clearly relevant: the survey underpins perception analysis, financial statements feed ratio work, and the scholarly articles and Adidas CSR report deepen conceptual understanding. Depth is evident (full financial statements, multi-page academic extracts) and the documents collectively give a range of stakeholder views (academic, corporate, primary data). This breadth and depth justify the top band for relevance.

All required presentation elements (word count, bibliography in alphabetical order, table of contents, labelled figures/tables, appendix with supporting documents) are present. Consequently, the highest presentation band is warranted.

Exactly five supporting documents are provided â survey results, two sets of financial statements, an academic CSR-brand-image article, an ESG/financial-performance paper and Adidasâ own CSR report. This sits comfortably within the 3â5 range required for the top band for âNumber of documentsâ.

Application is generally sound: ratios are calculated correctly, trends are interpreted, the perception map is plotted with survey data, and TBL findings are linked to Adidas campaigns. Commentary regularly links tool outputs to brand image and financial performance, indicating clear relevance. Minor weaknesses (e.g., limited critique of perception map bias) prevent a perfect score but overall application is effective.

The student uses multi-year financial data, full survey results and CSR metrics, providing a reasonable breadth of quantitative and qualitative evidence. Data are mostly appropriate and correctly used in calculations, though the survey sample is small and youthful, slightly limiting representativeness. These minor limitations keep the work in band 4 rather than 5.

Analysis links CSR actions to shifts in profitability, integrates the Kanye West controversy as a contextual variable, and ties perception-map findings to marketing implications. The reasoning is logical and mostly well-supported, though evaluative depth is sometimes surface-level (e.g., limited exploration of causality versus correlation for financial ratios). Hence âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ (band 4).

The research question is directly answered in the concluding section, with recommendations that stem from the analysed findings. The response is explicit rather than implied, fulfilling band-3 descriptors.

The selection of a perception map, triple-bottom-line and four financial ratios is appropriate and covers both qualitative and quantitative angles, but the range is still moderate and all tools are standard HL/SL syllabus items. There is no advanced strategic model (e.g., Ansoff, Porter) or triangulation tool that might extend selection breadth, so adequacy rather than outstanding effectiveness is demonstrated.

The conclusion synthesises survey insights and financial-trend discussion, explicitly stating that CSR enhances brand image and contributes to long-term financial gains. These statements are broadly consistent with the evidence presented. Minor over-generalisations (e.g., direct causal claims without full proof) are present but do not break consistency, justifying the top band for this strand.

Adidas remains the focal organisation throughout. Primary data (52-respondent survey), secondary data (three years of financials) and CSR initiatives are all directly tied back to Adidas when tools are applied. The analysis of perception misalignment, Kanye West impact and liquidity all show clear organisational linkage. Some sections (e.g., brief literature review) are generic, so full, seamless integration is not yet achieved, placing performance solidly in band 4 rather than 5.

The student consistently foregrounds sustainability as the key concept and shows a clear grasp of how it intersects with CSR, triple-bottom-line thinking and brand image. Sustainability is explicitly referenced in the research question, methodology, perception map axes and TBL analysis, demonstrating solid understanding. However, the integration is uneven; financial-ratio commentary occasionally drifts into generic profitability discussion without explicitly looping back to sustainability. Because analytical integration is strong yet not fully pervasive, band 4 is the best fit.

annotations.comment: The research question is clear but could be refined by specifying key metrics for brand image and financial performance to focus analysis.
The student consistently foregrounds sustainability as the key concept and links it to CSR throughout the introduction.
The âTable of Contentsâ heading is styled appropriately but would benefit from a subtitle or guidance for reader navigation.
The literature review paragraph is well-referenced but the OCR math artifacts disrupt citation clarity; revise formatting to standard footnote style.
Background on Adidas is concise but lacks critical analysis of how historical sustainability efforts inform current CSR strategy.
The table of contents spans the full range but numbering appears inconsistent; ensure section numbering aligns with headings.
The table caption appears below the table, disrupting flow; relocate it immediately above or within the table header.
Survey demographics reveal a skew toward juvenile respondents; extend reach to older cohorts to improve representativeness.
The analysis of purchase frequency lacks benchmarking or industry comparison, limiting evaluative depth.
Perception map introduction describes the tool but omits explanation of scale calibration; define axes' units and midpoints explicitly.
Interpretation assumes causality between sustainability commitment and perceived quality; can correlation alone justify this inference?
The methodology table includes justified primary research but lacks concrete strategies to mitigate acknowledged bias; propose stratified sampling or pilot testing.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Secondary research sources are listed but the table omits specific database names or journal details, reducing transparency.
Tool selection is appropriate and rooted in the syllabus, covering both qualitative and quantitative angles.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The TBL table effectively summarizes initiatives but lacks commentary on how people and planet metrics trade off against profit.
Analysis connects TBL findings to CSR strategy but misses explicit financial performance links in the people and planet dimensions.
The gross profit margin formula is applied correctly but lacks discussion of negative net profit margin for 2023; interpret the drop explicitly.
Presentation of the current ratio uses a fractional format; adopt the standard â1.27 : 1â notation for clarity.
The narrative attributes the NPM plunge to the Kanye West controversy without evidence quantification; avoid speculative causality.
The description of current ratio lacks clarification on denominator components; can you specify what comprises current liabilities?
Causal claims in the conclusion overstate the direct impact of CSR; temper with conditional phrasing or reference limitations.
Recommendations suggest using slogans but lack specificity; define targeted channels, audience segments, and message tone.
The limitations section begins well but omits discussion on potential financial statement biases and scope for broader data sources.
Supporting document figures on page 16 are unlabeled; add clear axis titles and legends for each survey chart.
Survey documentation lacks a clear statement of tool administration date and platform; include this to validate data currency.
The research paper summary omits author citations in-text; include parenthetical references to connect claims with sources.
CSR summary is descriptive but could be strengthened by integrating KPIs (e.g., reduction in carbon footprint) to quantify impact.
